title: Tirumala Venkateswara Temple
tagline: Tirumala Venkateswara Temple — spiritual town of Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: The Venkateswara Swami Temple—also known as Tirumala Temple, Tirupati Temple and Tirupati Balaji Temple—is a Hindu temple located in the hills of Tirumala, within Tirupati Urban Ma
overview.description: The Venkateswara Swami Temple—also known as Tirumala Temple, Tirupati Temple and Tirupati Balaji Temple—is a Hindu temple located in the hills of Tirumala, within Tirupati Urban Mandal, Tirupati district, Andhra Pradesh, India. The temple is dedicated to Venkateswara, a form of Vishnu, who is believed to have appeared on earth to save mankind from trials and troubles of Kali Yuga. Hence the site is also called Kaliyuga Vaikuntha and the deity is known as Kaliyuga Prathyaksha Daivam. Venkateswara is additionally known as Balaji, Govinda, and Srinivasa.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Sapthagiri
topPlaces.description: Sapthagiri, also called Edukondalu (ఏడుకొండలు) or Tirumala Hill, is a hill range situated in the hilly town of Tirumala, near Tirupati in the Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh, India. It is 853 m above sea level and is about 10.33 square miles (27 km2) in area.

topPlaces.name: Venkata (hill)
topPlaces.description: The Venkata hill (853 m) is part of the Seshachalam Hills, located in the Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh, India. Also known as Venkatadri, Venkatagiri or Venkatachalam, Venkatam, it is one of the seven peaks of the Tirumala Hill located in the temple town of Tirumala.

topPlaces.name: Govindaraja Temple, Tirupati
topPlaces.description: Sri Govindarajaswamy Temple is an ancient Hindu Vaishnavite temple situated in the heart of Tirupati city in Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh in India. The temple was built during 12th century and was consecrated in the year 1130 AD by Ramanuja as per the Vaikhanasa traditions, and the pancharatra jeeyar overseeing the divya prabandam recitation.

topPlaces.name: Kodandarama Temple, Tirupati
topPlaces.description: Sri Kodandaramaswamy Temple is a well known temple in the city of Tirupati located in Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh, India. The temple is dedicated to Hindu deity Rama, an incarnation of Vishnu along with Sita and Lakshmana.

topPlaces.name: Swami Pushkarini
topPlaces.description: Swami Pushkarini is a sacred temple tank located in Tirumala, Andhra Pradesh, India. Situated adjacent to the Venkateswara Temple, Tirumala, the tank covers approximately 1.5 acres and plays a central role in temple rituals, including the annual Teppotsavam (Float Festival) and the Chakrasnanam during the Brahmotsavams.

topPlaces.name: Varahaswamy Temple
topPlaces.description: The Sri Varahaswamy Temple, also called Bhu Varahaswamy Temple, is a Hindu temple dedicated to the god Varaha, situated at hill town of Tirumala in Tirupati, located in Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh state, India. The temple is situated on the northern premises of Venkateswara Temple, Tirumala, on the north-west corner of Swami Pushkarini.

topPlaces.name: Eparchaean Unconformity
topPlaces.description: Tirupati Eparchaean Unconformity, a notified National Geo-heritage Monument, is a major discontinuity of stratigraphic significance that represents a period of remarkable serenity in the geological history of the Earth i.e. sudden changes and discontinuity in the rock layers in Earth's crust.

topPlaces.name: Tirumala
topPlaces.description: Tirumala is a Hindu religious temple town in Tirupati district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is one of the neighbourhoods of the Tirupati city.

items.note: Sapthagiri, also called Edukondalu (ఏడుకొండలు) or Tirumala Hill, is a hill range situated in the hilly town of Tirumala, near Tirupati in the Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh, India. It is 853 m above sea level and is about 10.33 square miles (27 km2) in area.

The Venkata hill (853 m) is part of the Seshachalam Hills, located in the Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh, India. Also known as Venkatadri, Venkatagiri or Venkatachalam, Venkatam, it is one of the seven peaks of the Tirumala Hill located in the temple town of Tirumala.

Sri Govindarajaswamy Temple is an ancient Hindu Vaishnavite temple situated in the heart of Tirupati city in Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh in India. The temple was built during 12th century and was consecrated in the year 1130 AD by Ramanuja as per the Vaikhanasa traditions, and the pancharatra jeeyar overseeing the divya prabandam recitation.

Sri Kodandaramaswamy Temple is a well known temple in the city of Tirupati located in Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh, India. The temple is dedicated to Hindu deity Rama, an incarnation of Vishnu along with Sita and Lakshmana.

Swami Pushkarini is a sacred temple tank located in Tirumala, Andhra Pradesh, India. Situated adjacent to the Venkateswara Temple, Tirumala, the tank covers approximately 1.5 acres and plays a central role in temple rituals, including the annual Teppotsavam (Float Festival) and the Chakrasnanam during the Brahmotsavams.

The Sri Varahaswamy Temple, also called Bhu Varahaswamy Temple, is a Hindu temple dedicated to the god Varaha, situated at hill town of Tirumala in Tirupati, located in Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh state, India. The temple is situated on the northern premises of Venkateswara Temple, Tirumala, on the north-west corner of Swami Pushkarini.

Tirupati Eparchaean Unconformity, a notified National Geo-heritage Monument, is a major discontinuity of stratigraphic significance that represents a period of remarkable serenity in the geological history of the Earth i.e. sudden changes and discontinuity in the rock layers in Earth's crust.
Tirumala is a Hindu religious temple town in Tirupati district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is one of the neighbourhoods of the Tirupati city.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tirumala Venkateswara Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tirumala Venkateswara Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Tirumala Venkateswara Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tirupati Airport (22 km) and the nearest railway station is Tirupati Railway Station (10 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tirumala Venkateswara Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tirumala Venkateswara Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tirumala Venkateswara Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Tirumala Venkateswara Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Tirumala Venkateswara Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sapthagiri, Venkata (hill), Govindaraja Temple, Tirupati, Kodandarama Temple, Tirupati, Swami Pushkarini.
